questions.question: Which phase of quantum job execution typically has the most variable duration?
questions.explanation: Queue wait time is the most variable, ranging from milliseconds to hours depending on backend utilization, job priority, and system load. Other phases have more predictable durations.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using context managers for profiling phases?

options.text: They run faster than manual timing
They automatically handle start/stop timing even with exceptions
They are required by quantum frameworks

questions.explanation: Context managers (with statements) ensure that timing is properly stopped even if an exception occurs within the phase, providing robust and accurate measurements.

questions.question: When comparing transpilation at different optimization levels, what trade-off is typically observed?

options.text: Higher levels take longer but produce shallower circuits
Optimization level has no effect on compilation time

questions.explanation: Higher optimization levels (2, 3) apply more aggressive optimizations, taking longer to compile but typically producing circuits with reduced depth and gate count.

questions.question: Which timing function provides the highest resolution for profiling in Python?
questions.explanation: time.perf_counter() provides the highest resolution timer available on the system, making it ideal for measuring short code execution times. It includes time elapsed during sleep.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of running multiple benchmark iterations?

options.text: It makes the code run faster
It provides statistical confidence through mean and standard deviation
It is required by quantum hardware

questions.explanation: Multiple iterations allow calculation of mean and standard deviation, providing confidence intervals for timing measurements and accounting for variance from system load, caching, and other factors.

questions.question: Transpilation time scales linearly with circuit size regardless of optimization level.
questions.explanation: False. Transpilation complexity varies significantly with optimization level. Higher levels may have superlinear scaling due to more complex analysis passes like commutation analysis and resynthesis.

questions.question: When profiling VQE iterations, which phase typically dominates execution time?
questions.explanation: Expectation value estimation requires running quantum circuits multiple times (for different Pauli terms and shots), making it the dominant phase in VQE iterations.

questions.question: Which metrics are important when comparing backend performance?
questions.explanation: Mean execution time, standard deviation, and queue variability are all important for understanding backend performance and reliability. Consistency (low std) can be as important as raw speed.
